questions.scenario: You just started a fight. Your first GCD should be True Thrust.
questions.question: Which combo path grants Power Surge?
questions.explanation: Disembowel grants Power Surge (+10% damage). Always start with this combo to get the buff up first.

questions.scenario: Power Surge has 8 seconds remaining. You just finished Full Thrust combo.
questions.question: What should your next combo be?
questions.explanation: With 8 seconds left, use Chaos Thrust combo to refresh Power Surge before it falls off. Never let this buff drop.

questions.scenario: You need to use Chaos Thrust. Where should you stand?
questions.question: What's the correct position for Chaos Thrust?
questions.explanation: Chaos Thrust requires rear positional for bonus damage. Full Thrust requires flank. Missing = 100 potency loss.

questions.scenario: Your standard rotation alternates combos.
questions.question: What's the basic single-target pattern?
questions.explanation: Alternate: Chaos Thrust combo → Full Thrust combo → Chaos Thrust combo. This maintains both the DoT and Power Surge.

questions.scenario: Power Surge fell off mid-fight. Party is in burst phase.
questions.question: What's your priority?
questions.explanation: Power Surge is +10% to ALL damage. Even during burst, losing this buff is worse than delaying other actions. Get it back ASAP.

questions.scenario: High Jump is off cooldown. You just used a GCD.
questions.question: When should you use High Jump?
questions.explanation: Weave High Jump during the GCD window (after your GCD, before the next is ready). This prevents clipping.

questions.scenario: You just used High Jump. You have Dive Ready buff.
questions.question: What should you do with Dive Ready?
questions.explanation: Dive Ready enables Mirage Dive, which builds Eye gauge. Use it before your next High Jump or it's wasted.

questions.scenario: Boss has an AoE indicator under you. High Jump is ready.
questions.question: Should you use High Jump now?
questions.explanation: Jumps have ~0.8s animation lock where you can't move or act. Using during AoE means you'll get hit. Move first.

questions.scenario: You used High Jump. Where will you end up?
questions.question: What happens to your position?
questions.explanation: High Jump returns you to your original position. You don't fly away - this makes it safe for positional maintenance.

questions.scenario: You need to weave two oGCDs. High Jump + another ability.
questions.question: Why is double-weaving with High Jump risky?
questions.explanation: High Jump's animation lock is longer than most oGCDs. Double-weaving with it often causes GCD clip. Single-weave is safer.

questions.scenario: You have 1 Eye. Mirage Dive is available.
questions.question: What happens when you use Mirage Dive?
questions.explanation: Mirage Dive grants 1 Eye (max 2). You need 2 Eyes before using Geirskogul to enter Life of the Dragon.

questions.scenario: You have 2 Eyes. Geirskogul is ready.
questions.question: What happens when you use Geirskogul at 2 Eyes?
questions.explanation: At 2 Eyes, Geirskogul consumes 1 Eye and transforms you into Life of the Dragon for 20 seconds.

questions.scenario: You have 1 Eye. Geirskogul is ready.
questions.question: Should you use Geirskogul now?
questions.explanation: Using Geirskogul at 1 Eye just deals damage without entering Life of the Dragon. Build to 2 Eyes first.

questions.scenario: You have 2 Eyes. Mirage Dive is available but Geirskogul is on CD.
questions.question: Should you use Mirage Dive?
questions.explanation: At 2 Eyes, you're capped. Using Mirage Dive wastes an Eye. Wait for Geirskogul to consume an Eye first.

questions.scenario: You're about to enter a burst window. You have 0 Eyes.
questions.question: What's your Eye-building priority?
questions.explanation: Life of the Dragon (from 2 Eyes) unlocks Nastrond and Stardiver. Plan Eye building so you enter Life during burst.

questions.scenario: You're in Life of the Dragon. 18 seconds remaining.
questions.question: What does Geirskogul become during Life?
questions.explanation: During Life of the Dragon, Geirskogul transforms into Nastrond - same button, no Eye cost, powerful line AoE.

questions.scenario: Life of the Dragon active. Stardiver ready. 5 seconds on Life timer.
questions.question: Should you use Stardiver?
questions.explanation: Stardiver can only be used during Life of the Dragon. With 5s left, use it now or lose the opportunity.

questions.scenario: Life of the Dragon lasts 20 seconds. Nastrond shares Geirskogul's cooldown.
questions.question: How many Nastronds can you fit in one Life window?
questions.explanation: With 20s Life duration and Nastrond's ~10s CD, you can fit 3-4 Nastronds per Life phase.

questions.scenario: Lance Charge is active. You have 2 Eyes and Geirskogul ready.
questions.question: Is this a good time to enter Life?
questions.explanation: Entering Life during Lance Charge means your Nastronds and Stardiver get the +10% damage buff. Optimal timing.

questions.scenario: You used Stardiver. A new button appeared.
questions.question: What is Starcross?
questions.explanation: Starcross is a free follow-up that appears after Stardiver. Use it immediately for extra damage.

questions.scenario: Fight just started. Lance Charge is ready.
questions.question: When should you use Lance Charge?
questions.explanation: Use Lance Charge early in your opener to buff as many GCDs and oGCDs as possible. It's on 60s CD - don't hold it.

questions.scenario: Battle Litany is ready. Other DPS are using their 2-minute buffs.
questions.question: What does Battle Litany provide?
questions.explanation: Battle Litany grants +10% crit rate to the entire party. Coordinate it with other 2-minute raid buffs for maximum impact.

questions.scenario: Lance Charge is active. Battle Litany is also active. You have 2 Eyes.
questions.question: What should you prioritize?
questions.explanation: Double buff window is the perfect time for Life of the Dragon. Your Nastronds and Stardiver will hit much harder.

questions.scenario: Lance Charge (60s CD) and Battle Litany (120s CD) are both ready.
questions.question: How should you handle the different cooldowns?
options: Lance Charge on CD, Litany at 2m
questions.explanation: Use Lance Charge on cooldown (60s cycle). Battle Litany aligns with 2-minute raid buffs. They sync every other Lance Charge.

questions.scenario: Lance Charge has 5 seconds remaining. Boss is about to jump away.
questions.question: What should you prioritize?
questions.explanation: Squeeze out every buffed action before Lance Charge expires or the boss leaves. Jumps, Nastrond, whatever's available.

questions.scenario: Life Surge has 2 charges. You're about to use Heavens' Thrust.
questions.question: Should you use Life Surge?
questions.explanation: Life Surge guarantees a critical hit. Use it on Heavens' Thrust (highest potency GCD) for maximum value.

questions.scenario: Life Surge is at 2 charges. No combo finisher coming soon.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Use on any GCD to avoid cap
questions.explanation: At 2 charges, you're capped and losing potential charges. Better to use on a weaker GCD than waste the cooldown.

questions.scenario: Boss is against the wall. You can't reach the rear for Chaos Thrust.
questions.question: What should you use?
questions.explanation: True North removes positional requirements for 10s. Use it when you physically can't reach the correct position.

questions.scenario: True North has 2 charges. You're at flank but need rear.
questions.question: Should you use True North?
questions.explanation: If you can reach rear in time, walk there and save True North. Only use it when repositioning isn't possible.

questions.scenario: Life Surge ready. Battle Litany is active (+10% crit).
questions.question: Is Life Surge's guaranteed crit wasted during Litany?
questions.explanation: Life Surge guarantees crit, and Battle Litany increases crit rate. Together they still maximize damage - crit damage isn't diminished.

questions.scenario: You have 2 Firstminds Focus. Boss is far away.
questions.question: What can you use at range?
questions.explanation: Wyrmwind Thrust is ranged and uses Firstminds Focus. It's your disengage tool - use it when you can't reach the boss.

questions.scenario: You just used Heavens' Thrust. Firstminds Focus increased.
questions.question: How do you build Firstminds Focus?
questions.explanation: Firstminds Focus builds from Raiden Thrust and Heavens' Thrust (the upgraded combo finishers). 2 Focus = Wyrmwind Thrust.

questions.scenario: Chaotic Spring DoT has 5 seconds remaining. You're in the middle of Full Thrust combo.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: 5 seconds is enough time to finish your current combo, then start Chaos Thrust combo. Breaking combo loses more than a few DoT ticks.

questions.scenario: 3 enemies. Single-target combo is in progress.
questions.question: What's the AoE rotation?
questions.explanation: At 3+ targets, use the AoE combo: Doom Spike → Sonic Thrust → Coerthan Torment. It's higher total damage.

questions.scenario: Multiple enemies grouped. Dragonfire Dive and Stardiver both available.
questions.question: What makes Dragonfire Dive good for AoE?
questions.explanation: Dragonfire Dive deals AoE damage on landing. In multi-target situations, it's more valuable than in single-target.
